title_en: Thousands of Companies Are Driving China’s AI Boom. A Government Registry Tracks Them All
title_zh: 数千家企业正在推动中国的 AI 热潮。一个政府备案系统记录了所有企业
rubric_en: A Broad View of the Boom

paragraphs.en: When DeepSeek burst onto the global stage in January 2025, it seemed to appear out of nowhere. But the large language model was just one of the thousands of generative AI tools that have been released in China since 2023—and there’s a public archive of every single one of them.
paragraphs.zh: 当 DeepSeek 于 2025 年 1 月闪亮登上全球舞台时，它看起来像是从天而降。但这个大语言模型只是自 2023 年以来在中国推出的数千款生成式 AI 工具中的一个——而且每一款都有一个公开档案。

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 以下是中国重塑未来的 23 种方式。

paragraphs.en: The country’s top internet regulator, the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC), requires that any company launching an AI tool with “public opinion properties or social mobilization capabilities" first file it in a public database: the algorithm registry. In a submission, developers must show how their products avoid 31 categories of risk, from age and gender discrimination to psychological harm to “violating core socialist values.”
paragraphs.zh: 中国最高网络监管机构——中国国家互联网信息办公室（简称“网信办”）要求，任何推出具有“舆论导向属性或社会动员能力”的 AI 工具，必须先在公共数据库中备案：算法备案库。在提交材料中，开发者必须说明其产品如何避免 31 类风险，这些风险涵盖从年龄和性别歧视到心理伤害，再到“违反核心社会主义价值观”。

paragraphs.en: Applicants submit their filing to their local CAC (say, the Shanghai CAC for Shanghai-registered firms), which forwards applications to the central CAC for final approval. Only then is a tool publicly listed in the algorithm registry. While the European Union is pursuing a single, comprehensive AI Act, notes Matt Sheehan, a research scholar at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, China’s approach to regulation is more ad hoc, targeting specific algorithms and building up iterative standards. (The US has no comparable registration system or centralized regulatory agency.)
paragraphs.zh: 申请人向所在地的网信办提交备案（例如，上海注册公司向上海网信办提交），经其转交中央网信办后，再由中央网信办最终批准。只有这样，该工具才会在算法备案库中公开列出。卡内基国际和平基金会（Carnegie Endowment for International Peace）的研究学者 Matt Sheehan 指出，与欧盟正在推行一部统一且全面的《人工智能法案》不同，中国的监管方式更具“临时性和分步推进”特点，更多是围绕特定算法并逐步建立标准。美国则没有可比拟的统一登记制度或集中式监管机构。

paragraphs.en: Over time, the CAC has inadvertently created the most detailed map of a nation’s AI ecosystem anywhere in the world.
paragraphs.zh: 长期以来，CAC 不经意间绘制出了世界上最详尽的国家级人工智能生态图谱。

paragraphs.en: Open the CAC’s update from August 2024 and you’ll find DeepSeek listed as entry 152, a single row in a neatly packed table. Scroll through the table and you’ll find an AI that manages homestays and an AI that drafts patents. One assists ob-gyns in a Shanghai maternity ward; another helps manage state power grids. Kendra Schaefer and her colleagues at Trivium China, a Beijing-based policy consultancy, have been compiling the CAC’s updates into a comprehensive database, enriched with their own research.
paragraphs.zh: 打开 2024 年 8 月发布的 CAC 更新，你会看到 DeepSeek 被列为第 152 条，像表格里一行紧凑的数据。往下浏览表格，你会看到一个用于管理民宿的 AI，以及一个用于起草专利的 AI。一个帮助上海妇产科医生工作，另一个协助管理国家电网。Trivium China 的 Kendra Schaefer 与其同事——这是一家总部位于北京的政策咨询公司——一直在把 CAC 的更新整理成一个全面数据库，并结合他们自己的研究进行扩展。

paragraphs.en: Nearly 80 percent of China’s generative AI registrations are clustered in and around its top tech hubs—Beijing, Shenzhen, Shanghai, and Hangzhou. Each city has its strengths: Beijing’s elite universities, national labs, and political strength give it an edge in large-scale innovation; Shenzhen (in Guangdong) is home to a dense hardware supply chain and vast pool of engineering talent; Shanghai, close to multinationals, excels at commercialization; and Hangzhou (in Zhejiang) is fueled by Alibaba’s ecommerce empire.
paragraphs.zh: 中国近 80% 的生成式 AI 注册企业集中在其主要科技中心及周边：北京、深圳、上海和杭州。每个城市各有优势：北京凭借顶尖高校、国家级实验室和政治资源，在大规模创新方面占据先发优势；广东深圳拥有密集的硬件供应链和庞大的工程人才库；上海靠近跨国公司，尤其擅长商业化应用；而位于浙江的杭州则得益于阿里巴巴的电子商务帝国而持续腾飞。

paragraphs.en: But innovation spreads far beyond the coasts. Chongqing is positioning itself as an AI manufacturing and logistics node; and heavy state investment has helped Hefei, in Anhui Province, become known as “China’s speech valley” for its cluster of speech-recognition firms, including iFlyTek. Filings also originate in less obvious regions like Guizhou, China’s “Big Data Valley,” where massive data centers power Huawei’s Pangu model, and Inner Mongolia, where state enterprises are integrating AI into mining and agriculture.
paragraphs.zh: 但创新远不止于沿海地区。重庆正把自己定位为人工智能制造与物流枢纽；而安徽的合肥在大量国家投资下，也凭借包括科大讯飞在内的语音识别企业集群，逐渐被称为“中国语音谷”。备案还出现在更不显眼的区域，如贵州——中国的“数据谷”——那里巨型数据中心为华为的盘古模型提供算力支撑；以及内蒙古，那里国有企业正在将人工智能整合进采矿和农业领域。

paragraphs.en: In the Trivium dataset, state-linked listings—from state-owned enterprises to government-backed research institutes—make up 22 percent of filings. Many state-linked firms partner with Big Tech to build their AI: PetroChina, for example, teamed up with Huawei and iFlyTek to create oil and gas applications; State Grid used DeepSeek to build a model optimizing power grids.
paragraphs.zh: 在 Trivium 数据集中，具有国家背景的登记——从国有企业到政府支持的研究机构——占全部申报的22%。许多与政府有关联的公司与大型科技企业合作开发其人工智能：例如，中石油与华为和科大讯飞联合打造石油与天然气应用；国家电网则使用 DeepSeek 构建了用于优化电网的模型。

paragraphs.en: Foreign firms make up just 0.5 percent of filings. Ikea, for example, has a smart shopper algorithm that generates product recommendations. Yum China, the parent company that operates Kentucky Fried Chicken in China, listed a model that generates menus and promotional material.
paragraphs.zh: 外国企业仅占申报量的0.5%。例如，宜家推出了一个智能购物者算法，能够生成产品推荐；在中国运营肯德基的母公司 Yum China 提交了一款可生成菜单和促销材料的模型。

paragraphs.en: More than half of the listings in the algorithm registry are for what Schaefer calls cross-sector technologies. These range from foundational models to “general purpose” text generators to a wide array of multimedia tools—voice swappers, 3D renderers, image makers. “Nobody wants to be caught in a situation where they depend on a competitor’s technology,” Schaefer says. Unlike in the US, where OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind dominate the market, China’s competition to build foundational AI remains diverse and contested. But building these models is costly, and the market is beginning to consolidate. China’s six “AI tigers”—Moonshot, Minimax, Zhipu, Baichuan, 0.1AI, and Stepfun—are all backed by Alibaba or Tencent. ByteDance’s Doubao surpassed DeepSeek as China’s most popular chatbot, but its spot at the top is not assured.
paragraphs.zh: 在该算法名录中，超过一半的申报属于 Schaefer 所说的跨行业技术。这些技术覆盖基础模型、可用于“通用”用途的文本生成器，以及各种多媒体工具——包括语音转换器、3D 渲染工具、图像生成器。Schaefer 说：“没人愿意陷入依赖竞争对手技术的境地。”与美国不同，在美国市场上由 OpenAI、Anthropic 和 Google DeepMind 主导， 中国在构建基础人工智能方面的竞争格局仍然多元且激烈。但构建这些模型成本高昂，市场也开始出现集中趋势。中国的六大“AI老虎”——Moonshot、Minimax、智谱、百川、0.1AI 和智谱？——全部由阿里巴巴或腾讯提供支持。字节跳动的 Doubao 已超越 DeepSeek 成为中国最受欢迎的聊天机器人，但其领先地位并未得到完全保证。

paragraphs.en: While the giants duke it out for chatbot supremacy, startups are hard at work in every sector imaginable.
paragraphs.zh: 当巨头们在争夺聊天机器人王者时，创业公司正活跃于几乎所有可想象的行业。

paragraphs.en: Founder Derek Li says his 12-year-old company is leaps beyond the ed-tech competition. They “put wheels on a horse,” he says, bolting AI onto their existing stale software. Squirrel claims to diagnose knowledge gaps, measure progress, and adjust lessons in real time.
paragraphs.zh: 创始人Derek Li表示，他创办12年的公司已经远远超越了教育科技竞争。他说，他们是在现有老化的软件上“给马装上了轮子”，也就是把AI整合了进去。Squirrel声称能够诊断知识盲点、衡量学习进度，并实时调整课程。

paragraphs.en: When China banned for-profit tutoring in 2021, the company’s revenues collapsed overnight. It pivoted to licensing its platform to franchisees who also sold the company’s AI-powered tablets. Squirrel’s network includes more than 3,000 centers across China, serving 1.2 million students. Now, the company is eyeing expansion to the US.
paragraphs.zh: 2021年中国禁止营利性辅导后，该公司的收入一夜之间崩塌。随后，公司转向向加盟商授权其平台，这些加盟商也在销售该公司的AI驱动平板电脑。Squirrel的网络在中国已覆盖3000多个中心，服务120万名学生。如今，公司正寻求向美国扩张。

paragraphs.en: Li, who withdrew his sons from a private school in Shanghai so that they could be home-schooled on Squirrel’s platform, says that “in the future, teachers won’t teach knowledge.” Instead, he says, “they’ll become data analysts, understanding learning reports and students’ ability, and psychologists, understanding emotions and shaping their personalities.”
paragraphs.zh: Li将他的两个儿子从上海的一所私立学校转走，让他们在Squirrel平台上进行家庭学习，他说：“未来，教师不会再教知识。”取而代之地，他认为，“他们将成为数据分析师，理解学习报告和学生能力；也将成为心理学家，理解情绪并塑造个性。”

paragraphs.en: AI Kanshe (translated as “AI Sees Tongue”) is a traditional Chinese medicine startup that analyzes health through images of the tongue, palms, and face. The company was founded by Li Wenhua, a former employee of Yaoshi Bang, one of China’s earliest online pharmaceutical platforms. A longtime student of tongue and hand diagnosis, Li wanted to combine the diagnostic methods of traditional Chinese medicine with modern machine vision. The company serves both consumers and health practitioners across clinics, pharmacies, and some hospitals, offering tools to support diagnosis and decisionmaking. Its model is trained on more than 100,000 annotated images of tongues, hands, and faces.
paragraphs.zh: AI看舌（AI Kanshe，中文名意为“AI看舌”）是一家传统中医创业公司，通过舌象、手掌和面部图像来分析健康。该公司由前“药师帮”员工李文华创立，而“药师帮”是中国最早的在线药品平台之一。李长期研究舌诊和手诊，他希望将传统中医的诊断方法与现代机器视觉结合。公司面向门诊、药房和部分医院的消费者及医疗从业者提供服务，提供支持诊断与决策的软件工具。其模型基于十多万张带标注的舌、手、脸图像进行训练。

paragraphs.en: Founded in 2024 by Wu Song, a former Wall Street quant trader, Zhongtan Puhui Cloud Technology develops AI-driven tools for carbon accounting. The green transition, Wu says, still relies on cumbersome human labor that could be automated.
paragraphs.zh: 中潭普惠云科技成立于2024年，创始人为伍松，一位前华尔街量化交易员。伍松表示，绿色转型目前仍然依赖繁琐的人工劳动，而这些劳动本可以实现自动化。

paragraphs.en: Zhongtan Puhui builds AI agents that handle a number of carbon accounting tasks, including carbon footprinting and emissions audits. Its clients range from China Minmetals Group and DHL to small and medium-sized exporters in the Yangtze River Delta.
paragraphs.zh: 中潭普惠开发了处理多种碳核算任务的AI代理，包括碳足迹核算和排放审计。其客户既包括中国冶金集团和DHL，也包括长三角地区的中小型出口企业。

paragraphs.en: According to the company, its AI agents can pull real-time data directly from power plants, allowing them to complete a task that once took up to 30 days in 15 minutes—for a tenth of the price.
paragraphs.zh: 公司称，其AI代理可以直接从发电厂实时抓取数据，使一个过去可能需要长达30天才能完成的任务，缩短到15分钟，而且成本仅为原来的十分之一。

paragraphs.en: Zhongtan Puhui plans to expand into European countries. It has recently added English, French, Portuguese, and German versions and plans to sell the product on Amazon Cloud and Microsoft Azure.
paragraphs.zh: 中潭普惠计划扩展到欧洲国家。该公司最近新增了英文、法文、葡萄牙文和德文版本，并计划在亚马逊云和微软Azure平台上销售该产品。

paragraphs.en: Inspired after seeing Honda’s Asimo robot at a tech fair in Japan, Zhou Jian founded UBtech in 2012. He spent six years perfecting the robot’s servo actuators—the “joints” that enable movement—before unveiling the company's first robot, Alpha. In 2023, UBtech became the first humanoid company to list on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.
paragraphs.zh: 2012年，周健在日本一次科技展上见到了本田的阿西莫（Asimo）机器人后受到启发，创立了优必选（UBtech）。他花了六年时间完善机器人的伺服执行机构——也就是让其运动的“关节”——随后才推出公司的第一款机器人 Alpha。到2023年，优必选成为首家在香港联合交易所上市的人形机器人企业。

paragraphs.en: Based in Shenzhen, the company now produces robots for factories, schools, health care facilities, and homes. Its flagship Walker S series, designed for industrial and logistics work, is used by automakers like BYD and Zeekr; its Jimu and uKIT robotics kits teach students coding and AI; Alpha Mini, its child-friendly humanoid, is in nursery schools in Seoul. The company also develops robots for elder care and companionship, as well as consumer devices like smart vacuums and lawn mowers.
paragraphs.zh: 该公司总部位于深圳，目前生产用于工厂、学校、医疗机构和家庭的机器人。其标志性的 Walker S 系列面向工业和物流工作，由比亚迪、极氪等汽车制造商使用；其 Jimu 与 uKIT 机器人套件用于教授学生编码和人工智能；面向儿童的类人机器人 Alpha Mini 已在首尔的托儿所使用。公司还开发了用于老年照护与陪伴的机器人，以及扫地机器人和割草机等消费电子设备。

paragraphs.en: UBtech retrains existing foundational models, first through simulated environments, then in mock factory environments, before deployment in real production lines. The company currently sells to more than 40 countries, with roughly a third of its revenue coming from overseas markets.
paragraphs.zh: 优必选采用“先重训基础模型”的方法：先在仿真环境中训练，再在模拟工厂环境中训练，最后才部署到真实生产线上。该公司目前已向40多个国家销售产品，其中约三分之一收入来自海外市场。

paragraphs.en: XVerse is building lifelike virtual worlds for entertainment. The company was founded by former Tencent vice president Yao Xing. After leading Tencent to develop a Go-playing AI, Yao concluded that AGI progresses fastest when its goals are clear and measurable—as in the game of Go—but that most real-world challenges are open-ended. So in 2020, he left Tencent to found XVerse, aiming to build complex, simulated worlds where AI could learn, act, and evolve.
paragraphs.zh: XVerse 正在打造更逼真的虚拟娱乐世界。该公司由前腾讯副总裁姚星创立。在主导腾讯开发围棋 AI 后，姚星得出的结论是，通用人工智能的进展在目标清晰、可量化的场景下最快，比如围棋比赛；而现实世界中的多数挑战却是开放式的。于是2020年，他离开腾讯创办 XVerse，目标是构建复杂的仿真世界，让 AI 可以在其中学习、行动并进化。

paragraphs.en: In 2024, the company launched its flagship product, Saylo, which turns chat conversations into immersive AI-driven story videos. Users create personalized virtual characters and explore branching plots in real time. The app has 20 million users worldwide. The company also built its own large-language model, XVerse MoE, trained on diverse data sources including news, code, literature, and academic papers.
paragraphs.zh: 2024年，该公司推出了其旗舰产品Saylo，这是一款能将聊天内容转化为沉浸式、由AI驱动的故事视频的应用。用户可以创建个性化的虚拟角色，并实时探索分支剧情。该应用目前在全球拥有2000万名用户。公司还自主研发了大型语言模型XVerse MoE，训练数据源覆盖新闻、代码、文学和学术论文等多领域。

paragraphs.en: The company is also developing an AI-driven game, Zhaoyang Legend, set in a fantasy realm where players must ascend to the throne as a young crown prince and manage a kingdom. The game evolves dynamically based on players’ decisions, creating endless possible plots and endings.
paragraphs.zh: 该公司还在开发一款AI驱动的游戏《Zhaoyang Legend》（《朝阳传说》）。游戏设定在一个幻想世界，玩家将扮演年轻的皇太子并努力登上王位，进而治理王国。游戏会根据玩家的决策动态进化，产生几乎无限的剧情走向和结局。

paragraphs.en: BubblePal, a golf-ball-sized orb, transforms children’s toys into chatty companions. Clip it onto a stuffed Peppa Pig and the toy will talk back in Peppa’s voice. The device can switch between more than 40 personalities, from Disney’s Buzz Lightyear to the Monkey King, and speak in multiple languages and dialects, including French, German, Cantonese, and Sichuanese. A companion app lets parents set learning goals, such as encouraging more English conversation, and track weekly “growth reports” that analyze a child’s conversations, emotions, and interests. Since its launch last summer, more than 300,000 units have been sold.
paragraphs.zh: BubblePal是一颗高尔夫球大小的球体，可以把儿童玩具变成会说话的陪伴物。将其扣在一个绒毛小猪佩奇玩偶上，玩偶就会用小猪佩奇的声音进行对话。该设备可在40多种人格之间切换，从迪士尼的巴斯光年到孙悟空都可切换，并能说多种语言和方言，包括法语、德语、粤语和四川话。配套应用允许家长设定学习目标，例如鼓励孩子多说英语，并查看每周“成长报告”，分析孩子的对话内容、情绪状态与兴趣偏好。自去年夏季发布以来，已售出超过30万台。

paragraphs.en: The toy is made by Haivivi, a Chinese company founded in 2021 by Li Yong, a former Alibaba executive who helped develop the company’s smart speaker. Realizing that many of the smart speaker’s users were children, Li decided to make children’s toys, and in 2023 pivoted to AI companions. He relocated from Beijing to Shenzhen to tap into the city’s electronics supply chain.
paragraphs.zh: 该玩具由海维威（Haivivi）公司制造。海维威成立于2021年，由前阿里巴巴高管李勇创办，李勇曾参与公司智能音箱的研发。意识到许多智能音箱用户是儿童后，李勇决定转向儿童玩具，并于2023年转型为AI陪伴产品。随后他从北京搬到深圳，以便利用深圳完善的电子供应链。

paragraphs.en: According to the company, its models undergo strict data reviews and monitoring to prevent inappropriate speech. In addition to going through the rigorous algorithm registry vetting process, it follows the CAC rules for protecting minors’ data. BubblePal is also available in the US, Canada, and the UK, with plans for further expansion. Haivivi aims to add more popular characters such as Lucky Cat and develop product lines for young adults, like AI-powered tarot cards.
paragraphs.zh: 据该公司介绍，其模型会经过严格的数据审核与监测，以防止不当言论。在通过严谨的算法备案评估流程之外，它还遵守中国网信办关于未成年人数据保护的规定。BubblePal也在美国、加拿大和英国提供服务，并计划进一步扩展。Haivivi计划新增更多热门角色，如“Lucky Cat”，并开发面向年轻成人的产品线，例如由AI驱动的塔罗牌。

paragraphs.en: In recent years, the term chuhai—“to go overseas”—has become a buzzword as Chinese firms look abroad to escape slowing consumption and weak capital markets back home. China produces about a quarter of the world’s top 100 AI products by revenue, and most of them target overseas markets. A top earner for the Hangzhou company Glority, for example, is a plant-identification app called PictureThis, which is popular outside China.
paragraphs.zh: 近几年，“出海”一词已成为热门词汇，越来越多中国企业开始走向海外，以摆脱国内消费放缓和资本市场疲软。按收入计算，中国约占全球前100大AI产品的四分之一，而且其中大多数产品都面向海外市场。杭州公司Glority的头部盈利产品之一就是一款名为PictureThis的植物识别应用，该应用在中国以外也很受欢迎。

paragraphs.en: To avoid geopolitical trip wires, some Chinese tech companies have been distancing themselves from their roots by hiring foreign staff, setting up headquarters in Singapore or California, or leaving China altogether. A company called Butterfly Effect, for example, was founded in China and built a general-purpose AI agent called Manus. Shortly after the US established export controls on Nvidia chips, the company relocated to Singapore, laid off most of its Chinese staff, and scrubbed all of its content from Chinese social media.
paragraphs.zh: 为了避开地缘政治陷阱，一些中国科技公司正在试图与本土联系拉开距离，比如雇佣更多海外员工、在新加坡或加州设立总部，甚至彻底离开中国。例如，Butterfly Effect成立于中国，并开发了名为Manus的通用型AI代理。就在美国对英伟达芯片实施出口管制后不久，该公司搬迁到新加坡，裁撤了大部分中国员工，并清除了其在中文社交媒体上的全部内容。

paragraphs.en: Butterfly Effect’s ambiguous identity raises a deeper question: What does it mean to be a “Chinese” AI company?
paragraphs.zh: Butterfly Effect那种模糊的身份引出了一个更深层问题：什么才算“真正的”中国AI公司？
